headline: Jewish settlers in West Bank escalate harassment and terror campaign to annex Palestinian land
description: The death of a 14-year-old boy shot dead on Tuesday outside his school in Al-Mughayyir marks a new peak in an unprecedented spiral of violence in the West Bank. Targeted killings, destruction of farmland and expanding settlements have all intensified, largely ignored by political and diplomatic actors.

headline: EU gives final approval to €90-billion loan for Ukraine
In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
Meta plans to lay off about 8,000 employees as AI spending soars
description: The move comes as co-founder and chief executive Mark Zuckerberg makes a priority of delivering 'superintelligence' in a costly AI race against rivals including Amazon, Google, Microsoft and OpenAI. Reports on Thursday also indicated that Microsoft is looking to trim its ranks with voluntary buyouts of some US employees.

headline: Boris Pistorius, the man preparing Germany for war
description: The country's most popular politician for the past three years, who started serving as defense minister under Olaf Scholz, is set to unveil a military strategy review on Wednesday aimed at making the Bundeswehr the leading conventional army in Europe. Despite receiving unprecedented financial resources, the institution has been slow to reform.

headline: Léon Marchand prepares return to French pools: 'I always told myself the Paris Games were just one step'
description: The 23-year-old swimmer, who won four Olympic golds in 2024, is set to compete at the French Championships in Saint-Etienne in July, followed by the European Championships in August in Saint-Denis.

headline: 'Sudan, devastated by two warlords, faces the world's worst humanitarian crisis'
description: Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon reflects on more than three years of conflict in the African country of 50 million people.

description: The appeals trial over suspicions of Libyan funding for the 2007 presidential campaign began on March 16. The case is filled with a cast of shady figures.

headline: 'I was so exhausted, I started hitting myself. They laughed': Four Cuban asylum seekers recount their ordeal at Europe's borders
description: The European Court of Human Rights is expected to rule in the coming weeks on the pushbacks experienced by four Cubans at the border between Lithuania and Belarus, against the backdrop of a hybrid war with Russia.

headline: A neglected jewel, France's dilapidated National Museum of Natural History calls for help
description: The institution boasts strong visitor numbers and an international scientific reputation. But its head warns of catastrophic conditions, including mold in the collections, a flooded library, and cracks in the walls.
